title: audit — field classification
description: Field-level classification per foundation's five-tier taxonomy. Audit's `old_values` + `new_values` are the load-bearing surfaces — they may contain RAW PII (email, phone, medical record numbers, financial identifiers) because regulators require full diff context to reconstruct a mutation. Encrypted at rest via Postgres TDE. NEVER surfaced on the tenant wire without the audit.audits.reveal_raw_diff permission + a chain-of-custody meta-audit row.

regulated_minor: Audit.old_values (when auditable_type IN minor-affecting types: Athlete, AthleteGuardian, Consent) — treated with COPPA/parental-consent minimization on export
regulated_health: Audit.old_values (when auditable_type IN health-adjacent types: HealthNote (planned Wave 7), MedicalRecord (planned Wave 7)) — HIPAA-covered
regulated_financial: Audit.old_values (when auditable_type IN finance types: Transaction, Chargeback, Refund, Membership) — SOX + PCI-DSS covered
audit_log: AuditRetentionPolicy CRUD writes audit rows via HasAuditable (SELF-audit trail).
AuditExport row creation writes an audit row.
PurgeExpiredAuditsJob writes a summary meta-audit ('audit.purged') per tenant per run.
Raw-diff reveal writes a chain-of-custody audit row ('audit.raw_diff_revealed').
TenantErased writes an audit row for the erasure event itself + records the migration to compliance archive.
UserErased writes an audit row for the erasure + records the redaction cascade.

notes: Audit.old_values + Audit.new_values are RESTRICTED — they may contain raw PII per regulator requirement. Encrypted at rest via Postgres TDE. Hidden on the tenant wire by default; the audit.audits.reveal_raw_diff permission gates reveal + writes a chain-of-custody audit row on every reveal.

Audit.ip_address is CONFIDENTIAL — helpful for compliance investigations (source-IP reconstruction) but not PII-safe to broadcast. Hidden on the tenant wire; revealed only under audit.audits.reveal_raw_diff.

AuditExport.signed_url is CONFIDENTIAL — the URL is bearer-authorization for the export payload (24h expiry). Never broadcast; delivered exactly once via AuditExportReadyNotification to the requester.
AuditExport.manifest_sha256 is CONFIDENTIAL — used for chain-of-custody verification. Included in the notification payload so the requester can verify integrity of the downloaded file.

AuditRetentionPolicy.override_reason is INTERNAL — describes the compliance justification for the retention extension (e.g. 'SEC investigation 2026-Q3'). Not PII, but sensitive from a corporate-signaling perspective. Hidden on the platform-admin cross-tenant view; revealed on the tenant's own view for compliance-officer role.
Audit.user_agent + Audit.url are INTERNAL — helpful for forensic reconstruction but not for public consumption. Revealed on the tenant wire to compliance-officer role.
Audit.event carries the auditable transition name (e.g. 'created', 'updated', 'auth.login', 'transaction.approved'). It is public-safe.
